title: Spelling & Word Study
objective: Students will explore spelling & word study concepts including Suffixes (age 7+), Spelling Word Lists (age 7+), Apostrophes for possession (age 7+) and more.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Suffixes (age 7+)!

Spell words using productive suffixes (-ation, -ly, -ous) and less common sound-spelling correspondences (/ɪ/ as y, /ʌ/ as ou, endings sounding like /ʒə/, /tʃə/, /ʒən/, /ʃən/) introduced in the Year 3-4 programme
Let me show you something cool. Spell words with suffixes -ation (e.g., information, sensation), -ly (e.g., sadly, gently, happily), and -ous (e.g., famous, enormous, various)
Can you spell words with endings like "-tion," "-ous," or "-ly" correctly — for example, "nation," "famous," or "quietly" — without needing to look them up?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Suffixes (age 7+): Spell words with suffixes -ation (e.g., information, sensation), -ly (e.g., sadly, gently, happily), and -ous (e.g., famous, enormous, various) Spell words with less common vowel patterns: /ɪ/ as y (e.g., myth, gym), /ʌ/ as ou (e.g., young, touch, double)
Have you ever thought about Spelling Word Lists (age 7+)? Let's find out together!
Spell words from the statutory word list for Years 3 and 4, including commonly misspelt words that do not follow regular patterns
Time to try this out: Spell at least 20 words from the Y3-4 statutory list correctly (e.g., accident, believe, different, favourite, imagine)
Can you spell the tricky words on their school's Year 3 and 4 word list — like "enough," "believe," or "separate" — from memory when writing?

Great job! Today we learned about Spelling Word Lists (age 7+). Spell at least 20 words from the Y3-4 statutory list correctly (e.g., accident, believe, different, favourite, imagine) Identify tricky parts of statutory words and use a strategy to remember them (e.g., 'separate' has 'a rat' in the middle)
Let's explore Apostrophes for possession (age 7+)! Have you ever wondered about this?
Use the possessive apostrophe accurately with both regular and irregular plural nouns (e.g., the girls' bags, the children's toys), distinguishing singular from plural possession
Let me show you something cool. Place the apostrophe correctly in plural possessives: 'the dogs' kennel' vs 'the dog's kennel'
When you writes about something belonging to a group — like "the children's coats" or "the teachers' cars" — do they put the apostrophe in the right place?

Great job! Today we learned about Apostrophes for possession (age 7+). Place the apostrophe correctly in plural possessives: 'the dogs' kennel' vs 'the dog's kennel' Write possessive forms of irregular plurals correctly (e.g. 'children's', 'women's', 'mice's')
Let's discover the amazing world of Homophones!

Distinguish and correctly spell common homophones and near-homophones encountered at Y3-4 level (e.g., accept/except, affect/effect, brake/break, grate/great), including using the /eɪ/ sound spelt ei, eigh, or ey
Let's practice together! Spell pairs of homophones correctly in context (e.g., 'there/their/they're', 'brake/break', 'grate/great')
When you writes, do they use the right spelling for words that sound the same but mean different things — like "brake" (on a bike) and "break" (snap something)?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Homophones. Spell pairs of homophones correctly in context (e.g., 'there/their/they're', 'brake/break', 'grate/great') Choose the correct homophone to complete a sentence (e.g., 'The dog wagged its/it's tail')
Let's discover the amazing world of Prefixes (age 7+)!
Spell words with a range of prefixes (dis-, mis-, un-, re-, pre-, anti-, auto-, super-) understanding how each prefix modifies the root word's meaning without changing its spelling
Time to try this out: Spell words with prefixes dis-, mis-, re- correctly without altering the root (e.g., disappoint, misspell, return)
If you sees a word starting with "dis-," "mis-," or "pre-," can they work out what the word means and spell it correctly by thinking about the prefix?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Prefixes (age 7+): Spell words with prefixes dis-, mis-, re- correctly without altering the root (e.g., disappoint, misspell, return) Add prefixes un-, pre-, anti-, auto-, super- to root words and use the new word in a sentence (e.g., unhappy, preview, autograph)

lessons.title: Using a Dictionary to Check Spellings

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Using a Dictionary to Check Spellings!
Use the first two or three letters of a word to check its spelling in a dictionary; consult reference materials including beginning dictionaries to verify and correct spellings
Let me show you something cool. Locate a word in a dictionary using its first two or three letters (e.g., find 'beautiful' by looking up 'be-')
If you isn't sure how to spell a word, can they look it up in a dictionary by using the first two or three letters to find it quickly?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Using a Dictionary to Check Spellings: Locate a word in a dictionary using its first two or three letters (e.g., find 'beautiful' by looking up 'be-') Check and correct a misspelling by comparing with the dictionary entry (e.g., look up 'freind' → 'friend')
